title: pittosporum ferrugineum subsp. tinifolium
intro: Pittosporum ferrugineum subsp. tinifolium, also known as Tī Kōuka, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the Pittosporum genus and family Pittosporaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and providing relief from coughs and colds. The leaves and bark of the Tī Kōuka plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which have been used in traditional Māori medicine for generations.
